name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Directory Discovery (Unix)
notes:
This Building Block defines when a Unix command is run that discovers a large amount of directories. The commands detected by this rule are:
- "ls -d"
- "find -type d"
- "ls -r"
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Directory Discovery (Unix) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Command Execution Attempt
when the event matches Command Arguments (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches LOWER("Command Arguments") MATCHES '.*?("ls".*?"-[^\s-]{0,3}(d|r)|"find".*?"-type"\sa[0-9]+="d").*' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Directory Discovery (Unix) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Command Execution Attempt
AND when the event matches Command Arguments (custom) is not N/A
AND when the event matches LOWER("Command Arguments") MATCHES '.*?("ls".*?"-[^\s-]{0,3}(d|r)|"find".*?"-type"\sa[0-9]+="d").*' AQL filter query
